project: defender — an LLM SOC-analyst agent (python, pytest, uv)

gate.test: cd defender && uv run pytest tests/ learning/ -m "not llm and not live"
checks: the baseline-ratcheted custom lints under scripts/lint/lint_*.py (monkeypatch, unsafe-jsonl-io, raw-git-subprocess, unanchored-default, vulture, duplicate-helpers, …) — each blocks only on a NEW finding, and each documents an inline-suppression escape hatch in its own module
gate.notes: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is the source of truth — run what it runs; don't trust this list to stay current. Running in a git worktree? The shared `.venv` editable install points at the main checkout — prepend `PYTHONPATH=<worktree-root>` to the pytest call or you test the wrong tree.

tests.harness: defender/tests/e2e/_replay_harness.py — a new scenario is a few lines of `Turn(...)`, not fresh plumbing. Build on it rather than inventing parallel machinery.
tests.idioms: Fakes enter through the entry point's injection seams (a `deps` parameter, a constructor argument), never `monkeypatch.setattr` — CI ratchets new setattr sites (`scripts/lint/lint_monkeypatch.py`).

conventions.dangerLens: adversarial input — alert data is attacker-influenced by definition, and malicious input is well-formed on purpose. Use this as write-tests' fourth enumeration lens unless the change's danger zone is clearly elsewhere (environment/resource, or concurrency when the design admits parallel invocation).
